name: Puerto de Peñanegra y Chia desde San Martin - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos — circular desde Piedrahíta
region_intro: Road cycling around Piedrahíta is characterized by its mountainous terrain, featuring significant elevation changes and challenging ascents. The region includes prominent mountain passes such as Peña Negra and Alto del Reventón, offering demanding climbs and descents. Valleys like the Corneja provide varied landscapes for road cyclists. The area's topography is suitable for those seeking routes with substantial elevation gain. Best road cycling routes around Piedrahíta The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de Peñanegra y Chia desde San Martin - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos — circular desde Piedrahíta, a 51.2 miles (82.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 41 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant climbs through the Sierra de Gredos. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peña Negra and Chía Pass Loop from Piedrahíta, a difficult 40.7 miles (65.5 km) path. This route navigates two major mountain passes, offering sustained ascents and panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Puertos de Peñanegra y Alto del Reventón — circular desde Piedrahíta, a 50.1 miles (80.7 km) trail leading through mountain passes and high-altitude landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around Piedrahíta is defined by mountain passes, valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for moderate to advanced cycling abilities, with many routes featuring substantial elevation gain. The routes in Piedrahíta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Piedrahíta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes around Piedrahíta?
A: Given the mountainous terrain, most road cycling routes around Piedrahíta are moderate to difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and challenging ascents. There are no routes specifically categorized as "easy" in this area, making it more suitable for experienced road cyclists.

Q: What kind of distances and elevation gains can I expect on road cycling routes in Piedrahíta?
A: Road cycling routes in Piedrahíta typically range from 40 to 50 miles (65 to 80 km) with substantial elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063028971/puerto-de-penanegra-y-chia-desde-san-martin-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Peñanegra y Chia desde San Martin&lt;/a&gt; route covers 51.2 miles (82.4 km) with over 1800 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315444519/pena-negra-and-chia-pass-loop-from-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra and Chía Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 40.7 miles (65.5 km) with over 1300 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Piedrahíta?
A: Due to the region's mountainous topography and the prevalence of routes with significant elevation gain and technical descents, Piedrahíta is generally not ideal for family-friendly road cycling with young children or beginners. The routes are primarily geared towards moderate to advanced cyclists.

Q: What are some of the more advanced cycling routes in Piedrahíta?
A: Piedrahíta offers several challenging routes for advanced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062415266/puertos-de-penanegra-y-alto-del-reventon-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertos de Peñanegra y Alto del Reventón&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, is a difficult 50.1-mile (80.7 km) trail that leads through multiple mountain passes and high-altitude landscapes, demanding strong climbing abilities.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Piedrahíta?
A: The best time for road biking in Piedrahíta is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the weather is more stable. Summers can be very hot, especially in the valleys, while winters can bring cold temperatures and snow to the higher mountain passes.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the cycling routes in Piedrahíta?
A: Yes, many routes in Piedrahíta offer panoramic views, especially when traversing mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5216726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The region's mountainous terrain provides numerous opportunities for breathtaking vistas of the Sierra de Gredos and surrounding valleys.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Piedrahíta?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Piedrahíta, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning mountain scenery, and the sense of accomplishment from conquering passes like Peña Negra and Alto del Reventón.

Q: Are there road bike loop routes available in Piedrahíta?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Piedrahíta are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315444519/pena-negra-and-chia-pass-loop-from-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra and Chía Pass Loop from Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315486307/corneja-valley-and-el-miron-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corneja Valley and El Mirón Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Along the road cycling routes, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5216726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Pass&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature, offering stunning views. You might also pass by historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponseca Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4448740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Bonilla de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, depending on your chosen route.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Piedrahíta?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes available around Piedrahíta, offering a wide range of options for cyclists looking to explore the mountainous terrain and valleys of the region.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty road cycling routes in Piedrahíta?
A: Yes, while many routes are difficult, there are also moderate options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315486307/corneja-valley-and-el-miron-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corneja Valley and El Mirón Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 35.2-mile (56.6 km) route with over 780 meters of elevation gain, offering a less strenuous but still engaging ride.
tips: It is 1,663 meters above sea level and connects the Corneja Valley (Valdecorneja) with the Alto Valle del Alberche through a local road that connects the towns of Navacepedilla de Corneja and San Martín de la Vega del Alberche. It separates the Sierra de Villafranca and the massif of La Serrota.

It is 1,767 meters above sea level and connects the Alto Alberche valley with that of Alto Tormes, through a local road that connects the towns of San Martín de la Vega del Alberche and Barajas, annexed to Navarredonda de Gredos, in whose municipal area it is located. located the coronation of the same.

In this part of the Sierra de Gredos, more specifically on the northern side of the mountain range, we are shown some beautiful slopes full of fauna and flora. The road takes you to the heart of the natural park where it is always a pleasure to cycle through these valleys.

The port of Peña Negra, on its north face, starts from the town of Piedrahita, at the turnoff to Pesquera. Already in the same streets of Piedrahita the slopes stabilize around 5-6%, which will be the usual trend. Because Peña Negra, as can be seen in the altimetric profile, is a really regular port, except for a few short breaks in the second half.
